page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 16 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average performance tier across most standardized tests. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a single-core stress-ng CPU score of 1860.49 and a multi-core score of 31104.89. In memory operations, the instance records a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1685.59 GB/sec, which drops to 151.74 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 66.82 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by hardware flags including AVX-512 and AES, reflected in a Passmark CPU encryption score of 29225.6.

For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates a strong performance tier in text generation for small language models, reaching 310.19 tokens/sec, though prompt processing for the same model remains in the average tier at 2214.03 tokens/sec. Medium model text generation operates at an average rate of 18.57 tokens/sec. Given the 1:2 memory-to-vCPU ratio and the absence of local storage, this instance is structured for compute-bound tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and moderate database operations rather than memory-heavy or storage-intensive workloads.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.8xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, it maintains a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Network performance is anchored by a 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmark data places the instance in the average tier for most compute and memory tests, including a Passmark CPU score of 36657.12 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 31104.89. Notably, it achieves strong tier performance in text generation for small language models at 310.19 tokens/sec. Lacking local storage, it is designed for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering balanced mid-tier performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7t.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 16 Gbps baseline bandwidth.
tagline: A dedicated 32-core Intel Xeon compute instance with a 1:2 memory-to-core ratio and 16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated physical Intel Xeon 8369B cores running at 2.7 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
16 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with zero local storage
Strong-tier text generation performance for small language models
Suited for software compilation, web serving, and database operations
